creator: linimon@lonesome.com
text:
On Sat, Mar 27, 2010 at 09:30:09PM +0000, olli hauer wrote:
>  I don't know why the issue is assigned to freebsd-ports-bugs@FreeBSD.org
>  instead to me (maintainer)

Initially the Subject line was:

  "spamd port insists on fdescfs mount"

which is not enough information to get the attention of the auto-nagger.

In general, when a FreeBSD developer comes along and uses GNATS to manually
fix the synopsis to be the correct:

  "mail/spamd port insists on fdescfs mount"

they're then supposed to forward the corrected message to the special email
address "gnats-aa@FreeBSD.org".  It looks like this didn't happen in this
case.  (I'm not sure if it was me, or someone else.)

> even half of the conversation is missing on the web-frontend

The only way to get followups into GNATS is to Cc: "bug-followup@FreeBSD.org"
(I think there is another equivalent, perhaps gnats@FreeBSD.org).  It is not
necessary to Cc: any of the mailing lists; that will happen automagically.

If GNATS doesn't see the email, GNATS doesn't do anything with it :-)

(Also, followup email must be prefixed with "Re: ports/12345: " (or whatever
number) for it to be handled correctly.  AFAICT the rest after that is
irrelevant, but that part must be literally correct or it will create a
new PR which will then have to be fixed by hand.)

mcl
